title: NFR assessment
description: E108-S1 / AF-2026-06-03-1 / Test17 D-2/D-6. Backing JSON Schema for the `nfr-assessment` artifact type so /gaia-val-validate can enforce structural invariants, not merely route the type. The nfr-assessment artifact is a prose-markdown document with a YAML frontmatter metadata block (grounded in the on-disk corpus instance `.gaia/artifacts/test-artifacts/strategy/nfr-assessment.md` plus the /gaia-nfr SKILL Step structure). The shared validate-artifact-schema.sh helper (E108-S5) extracts the frontmatter for *.md instances and validates it against THIS schema; the prose body is not machine-validated by a frontmatter schema, so the eight canonical H2 sections it MUST carry are documented in `x-required-sections` below for the document-ruleset and reviewer. Pragmatic, grounded shape — required metadata is pinned, but additional frontmatter keys (e.g. `framework_version`) are permitted.

template.description: Template identifier. MUST be `nfr-assessment` — the slug that selects the document-specific ruleset and aligns with the on-disk artifact filename per the /gaia-val-validate artifact_type enum.

version.description: Template/document version (semver-shaped in the corpus, e.g. `1.0.0`). Bumped when the assessment is re-run under a newer template.

date.description: Assessment date. ISO-8601 `YYYY-MM-DD` per the corpus instance.

project.description: Human-readable project name the assessment covers (e.g. `GAIA Framework`).

framework_version.description: Optional. The GAIA framework version at assessment time, when recorded (present in the corpus instance). Informational provenance.

x-required-sections.description: The eight canonical H2 (`##`) sections the prose body MUST carry, grounded in the corpus instance + /gaia-nfr Step structure. Validated by the document-ruleset / reviewer, not by this frontmatter schema.
